intent: a personal dashboard for tracking long-term health experiments

body.intent: a personal dashboard for tracking long-term health experiments

draft.title: a personal dashboard for tracking long-term health experiments
draft.summary: Drafting your prototype from the swipes…
draft.html:
a personal dashboard for tracking long-term health experiments

Building your first draft…

data.intent: a personal dashboard for tracking long-term health experiments

facade.hypothesis: Users tracking long-term health experiments respond better to gentle, organic visual feedback (curves, gradients, warmth) than hard numeric counters
facade.label: Soft progress rings
facade.acceptImplies: Lean into fluid, rounded, tactile feedback. Curves over edges. Warmth over clinical precision.
facade.rejectImplies: Users want clarity and efficiency. Try sharp numbers, clean grids, minimal embellishment.

facade.hypothesis: Users prefer seeing individual metrics in large type over comparative/contextual data that requires reading
facade.label: Big numbers, small context
facade.acceptImplies: Dashboard should lead with single KPIs (weight, steps, sleep hours) in prominent display, minimize surrounding narrative or benchmarks
facade.rejectImplies: Dashboard should prioritize contextualized metrics (trends, ranges, comparisons to baseline) even if individual numbers are smaller or less prominent

facade.hypothesis: Users prefer supportive, conversational tone over clinical data presentation
facade.axisTargeted: Voice and Personality: Friendly vs Professional
facade.label: Warm encouragement
facade.acceptImplies: Design voice should feel like a supportive friend checking in on progress, use affirmations and celebratory language, soften numbers with context
facade.rejectImplies: Voice should lean clinical, data-forward, or neutral—minimize emotional tone, let metrics speak for themselves

facade.hypothesis: Users prefer fluid transitions between dashboard views over instant page swaps
facade.axisTargeted: Transition speed and easing — does the interface feel fluid and animated, or snappy and instant?
facade.label: Smooth slide in
facade.acceptImplies: User wants motion-forward design; transitions should ease data views, charts, and panels into place with momentum
facade.rejectImplies: User prefers instant, no-motion design; transitions should snap without animation or keep interaction crisp and snappy

evidence.hypothesis: Users tracking long-term health experiments respond better to gentle, organic visual feedback (curves, gradients, warmth) than hard numeric counters
evidence.implication: Lean into fluid, rounded, tactile feedback. Curves over edges. Warmth over clinical precision.

facade.hypothesis: Users prefer a persistent left navigation paired with card-based content layout for scanning multiple health experiments at once
facade.label: Sidebar with cards
facade.acceptImplies: User gravitates toward modular, scannable layouts with stable navigation anchors; likely wants quick overview + drill-down pattern
facade.rejectImplies: User may prefer full-width immersion, tabbed navigation, or list-based sequential scanning over spatial card grids

axes.label: Whether user prefers minimal clinical aesthetics vs. warm, human-centered visual design for health data
Whether user wants data-dense dashboard layout vs. progressive disclosure with one metric at a time
axes.poleA: All at once or step by step?

axes.label: Whether user responds to motivational/encouraging tone vs. neutral, factual reporting voice
Whether user wants celebratory, gamified feedback vs. calm, meditative tracking environment
Whether user prefers raw numbers and charts vs. narrative summaries and insights
Whether user wants instant feedback on actions vs. slow, contemplative animations and transitions

scout_assignments.reason: Cold start: first question for Iris

scout_assignments.reason: Cold start: first question for Prism

scout_assignments.reason: Cold start: first question for Lumen

scout_assignments.probe_axis: Whether user wants celebratory, gamified feedback vs. calm, meditative tracking environment
scout_assignments.reason: Cold start: first question for Aura

scout_assignments.probe_axis: Whether user prefers raw numbers and charts vs. narrative summaries and insights
scout_assignments.reason: Cold start: first question for Facet

scout_assignments.probe_axis: Whether user wants instant feedback on actions vs. slow, contemplative animations and transitions
scout_assignments.reason: Cold start: first question for Echo

facade.hypothesis: Users tracking health experiments prefer gentle, approachable UI over clinical sharpness
facade.axisTargeted: Roundedness vs. sharpness in component design
facade.label: Soft rounded corners
facade.acceptImplies: Design system leans toward friendliness and accessibility; soften card edges, buttons, input fields; creates calm, less intimidating feel for health-focused app
facade.rejectImplies: Design system favors clarity and precision; keep edges crisp and defined; suggests clinical, no-nonsense aesthetic that may feel more trustworthy for data tracking

facade.hypothesis: Users tracking long-term health experiments prefer seeing time-remaining (or milestone-distance) in soothing, ambient format rather than urgent or motivational framing
facade.label: Calm countdown timer
facade.acceptImplies: Design milestone markers and progress timelines with soft typography, breathing animations, and patience-oriented language. Avoid countdown pressure or gamified urgency.
facade.rejectImplies: Users need momentum and drive—lean into energetic language, fast-moving progress bars, and achievement milestones that trigger motivation and excitement.
